contract.name: v2/disaster/agency/spending.md
contract.description: Aggregates disaster and emergency spending by agency with optional award-only metrics and paginated sorting controls.

filter.description: Filter object; every request must identify which disaster emergency fund codes to aggregate and can optionally narrow awards by type or keyword.
filter.constraints: `def_codes` must be a non-empty array of valid uppercase DEFC codes (invalid values yield 400; empty arrays yield 422). `award_type_codes`, when provided, must be a non-empty array drawn from ['-1','02','03','04','05','06','07','08','09','10','11','A','B','C','D','IDV_A','IDV_B','IDV_B_A','IDV_B_B','IDV_B_C','IDV_C','IDV_D','IDV_E']; supplying an empty array or non-array raises 422/400. `award_type_codes` is ignored when `spending_type` is 'total' but triggers child agency breakdowns for award views. `query` accepts arbitrary strings (very long strings succeed) and only affects `spending_type='award'`; total spending ignores it. Additional shared-search keys such as `time_period` or `agencies` are accepted but ignored without warnings.

spending_type.description: Selects whether totals reflect all budgetary resources ('total') or only award-linked spending ('award').

pagination.description: Optional pagination and sorting controls applied to the agency results array.
pagination.constraints: `limit` must resolve to an integer between 1 and 100 inclusive (values outside return 422). `page` must be an integer ≥ 1 (0 returns 422). `order` accepts only 'asc' or 'desc' (other values return 400). For `spending_type='total'`, `sort` may be one of ['id','code','description','award_count','total_budgetary_resources','obligation','outlay']; for `spending_type='award'`, valid values are ['id','code','description','award_count','obligation','outlay'] and requesting 'total_budgetary_resources' returns 400. Unsupported sort strings also return 400. Certain award-view sorts (e.g., 'id' or 'code') complete but emit warnings in `messages` and fall back to description ordering.

totals.description: Aggregate metrics for the requested view. Always includes numeric `obligation` and `outlay`. `total_budgetary_resources` is present only for `spending_type='total'`. `award_count` is present only for `spending_type='award'`. When filters match nothing, values return as 0.

results.description: Array of agency aggregate objects. Each item provides `id` (integer or null), `code` (string), `description` (string), nullable numeric `award_count`, `obligation`, `outlay`, and `total_budgetary_resources`. For total spending, `total_budgetary_resources` is populated; for award spending it is null. Every item includes a `children` array (often empty); when populated (observed after supplying `award_type_codes`), each child includes `id`, `code`, `description`, `award_count`, `obligation`, and `outlay` but omits `total_budgetary_resources`.

page_metadata.description: Pagination block with `page`, `total`, `limit`, `next`, `previous`, `hasNext`, and `hasPrevious`. `next`/`previous` are null at the respective ends. Empty result pages retain accurate `total` counts.

messages.description: Optional array of warning strings. Observed when the service cannot honor the requested sort (e.g., award view sorted by 'id' or 'code'); results fall back to description ordering while including a notice.

results.description: Department of Health and Human Services
Department of Health and Human Services

messages: Notice! API Request to sort on 'code' field isn't fully implemented. Results were actually sorted using 'description' field.

quirks: Keyword filter `query` is ignored for `spending_type='total'`, returning the same aggregates regardless of the keyword.
Passing `award_type_codes` introduces child agency rows and can emit fallback warnings when combined with unsupported sorts.
Pagination requests beyond the available data return empty `results` while preserving accurate `page_metadata.total` counts.
Shared search filters such as `time_period` and `agencies` are silently ignored, so the response mirrors the unfiltered dataset.
risks: Consumers may assume keyword or other shared filters apply to total spending, but the API ignores them without warning.
`results.id` can be null; treating it as a stable identifier can break client-side joins or caching.
Award responses can include negative obligations or outlays, so downstream sums must handle sign correctly.
Error responses alternate between HTTP 400 and 422 with only a `detail` string; clients should not expect structured error payloads.
Sort requests that the backend cannot honor either fail with 400 or succeed while silently substituting ordering, signaled only via `messages`.
gaps: Unclear which, if any, additional shared filters (locations, recipients, etc.) have real effect; tests show some are ignored but coverage is incomplete.

probes.notes: Minimal request succeeds and returns five agencies without keyword filtering.
Award view returns award counts and omits total_budgetary_resources while totals include award_count.
Adding award_type_codes yields child rows and emits a warning that sort order fell back when requesting 'id'.
Pagination limit trims results to two entries and supplies a next-page pointer.
Unsupported sort strings trigger HTTP 400 with an explicit detail list.
Limit enforces a minimum of 1 via 422.
Keyword filter leaves total spending results unchanged, confirming it is ignored in that mode.
Adding a time_period filter leaves totals unchanged, indicating the backend ignores it.
Award view rejects `total_budgetary_resources` as a sort option despite documentation listing it.

mismatches: Docs declare Result.id as a string, but live responses supply integers and sometimes null.
Docs describe `filter.query` as a keyword filter for all views, yet total spending ignores it entirely.
Docs instruct that `award_type_codes` be used only with `spending_type='award'`, but the API accepts them with 'total' and produces unchanged results without warnings.
Docs claim Result children mirror the parent schema, but award children omit `total_budgetary_resources`.
Docs list 'total_budgetary_resources' as a valid sort choice, yet award spending requests reject it with HTTP 400.
gaps: Unclear which additional shared filters (locations, recipients, etc.) are respected; only time_period and agencies were tested and found inert.
risks: Clients may believe ignored filters (keyword, time_period, agencies) restrict totals when they do not.
`results.id` can be null, so downstream systems must not rely on it as a primary key.
Award aggregates may return negative obligations or outlays, which can distort naive rollups.
Error handling varies between 400 and 422 with only a `detail` string, complicating machine parsing.
Unsupported sort requests may either fail or silently downgrade ordering, requiring consumers to monitor `messages`.
